### Post by dannyboy79 on 2007-02-22
i just used envy for the first time and since I am doing this thru ssh from work, I don't know if it's working yet but I can say that when I did ps aux, it shows gdm is working and dmesg shows 9746. 

Some history on why I went with Envy. Originally I was just using the nvidia driver within dappers repos with my 6200 but in Sept of 2006 I decided to get the latest and greatest. so i first downloaded the 9629 NVIDIA installer from nvidia's website cause I thought that was the way to do it BUT I don't think I used that because for 1 thing my dmesg always said 9631 and when I looked to use the uninstaller when I wanted to go to 9746 I didn't have a folder with an installer file in it. I only had a file called blah blah.run. So I am guessing that I had used albertomilone's 1st repo for 9631 for dapper which was deb [http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit](http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/nonlegacy/32bit) binary/    back in sept of 2006. Well, I updated my kernel and there went my nvidia driver so I needed to install it again. I looked  at [www.albertomilone.com](www.albertomilone.com) and noticed that he changed the repo for the lattest driver so I updated my repo to 
deb [http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/latest/32bit](http://www.albertomilone.com/drivers/dapper/latest/32bit) binary/     
and did the update and dist-upgrade but x failed stating the API-mismatch and somehting about the nvidia kernel image blah blah. So I investigatyed this and found I needed to uninstall the driver first before updating. Well, I never used the installer, so how can I uninstall the previous driver?? Needless to say I was back and forth between albert's website and the ubuntu forums and thought the easiest way would be to try out envy, use the uninstall function, and then the install function. Well, like I said, since I did all this from work thru ssh, I can't tell for sure if it worked but I think so since ps aux | grep gdm shows gdm is running which I believe is the graphical interface for logging in so I beleive it worked. 

My question now is that during the install using Envy, I saw this error:
unmet dependencies" xserver-xorg-dev: depends on libdrm-dev but will not be installed.
Does this matter?

Also, why did you, tseliot, change your repo for the latest dapper nvidia driver? If i originally used your repo to install the 9631 and then did a kernel update, why did a sudo aptitude update && sudo aptitude dist-upgrade NOT work?? I am only curious? Also, now that I used envy, can I erase your repo from my sources.list? How come you don't put your envy script in your dapper repo? THanks for any answers you can provide. SSL (sorry so long)


edit: IT DIDN'T WORK, I got home and found that I had that blue screen telling me that xserver failed and it was asking me if I want to read the failure report or something or other. I read it and it was saying that the nvidia module wasn't being loaded?? That's weird, I thought the envy script was suppose to install the module? So I simply did a sudo aptitude install nvidia-glx and everything is a-ok now!! Yeah. Still wierd about that Envy script, why wouldn't it have installed the module itself? Maybe tseliot can can answer this.

### Post by TheFuzzy0ne on 2007-02-28
Hi everyone.

I am running Kubuntu Dapper Drake, and I used Envy before with great success. However, I recently needed to reinstall my OS from scratch, and I just can't get envy to work. 

The latest version causes the login screen to appear for a split second, and then it disappears for about 5 seconds. It does this continously, and won't even let me use CTRL+ALT+F1(-6), to get into the console. 

I tried to install the drivers manually through Envy, and went for option 2, which is not the latest. This time it kind of works... I get the login splash screen, but I am unable to see the actual login dialog. The wierd thing is, it's actually there, just not visible. When I slowly move the cursor down the center of the screen, an changes to a caret twice. I can click and type my password, and the caret returns to a cursor, which logs me in. However, the desktop is still not visible, only the blue background. The only way I can get it to either show the login prompt, or the desktop, is to do CTRL+ALT+F1(-6), then login. Once logged in, I don't need to run X or KDE, I simply do CTRL+ALT+F7, and the login prompt is there.

I tried option 3 in the manual installation list, and this seems to work, although it crashes at random stages before I can actually get to my desktop.

When I ran the Envy script previously, after I'd last installed Kubuntu, everything worked great first time. Sadly, I was too stupid to make a not of the version of Envy it was, but if I recall correctly, it didn't give the option to install/uninstall the ATI drivers, nor did it give the option to manually install any drivers. I am fairly new to Linux, although I've been using it for a few months. I am quite reliant on scripts, although I can troubleshoot most simple problems, but this one is completely beyond me. I've tried lots of other things (too much to list here), and I've gotten absolutely nowhere. Is it possible to download the previous versions, and will will it download the correct NVidia driver, or would it get the latest?

I have absolutely no clue what driver I need to install, so any pointers would be appreciated. I have tried the manual installation procedure found at [http://albertomilone.com/latest_nvidia_udsf.html](http://albertomilone.com/latest_nvidia_udsf.html) , and all I got was a the kubuntu spashscreen when I rebooted, instead of the login screen. I also got a whole host of errors, such as memory allocation errors and window errors. I'd also like to point out that I have spent 3 days on this, and I have installed Kubuntu 11 times. The disk passed the defect testing program, so I would assume there's nothing wrong with the install.

Thanks in advance for any assistance you may be able to offer.

Daz.

Edit 1: My AGP card is the Nvidia 5200FX, and I am running the latest stable K7 kernel for the Athlon XP (2.4.27-2-k7)

Thanks again for a great utility.
You have my vote for making this a standardized NVIDIA installer for Ubuntu !

### Post by yabbadabbadont on 2007-05-02
> **N o i r said:**
> So, why exactly Feisty is supported up to 2008 while 6.06 is supported up to 2009? Knowing nothing of both, I choosed to install 6.06 solely upon this detail.

Because Dapper Drake (6.06) is an LTS (long term support) release while Feisty and Edgy are just normal releases.

### Post by FEXOR on 2007-11-04
Please anyone add this guide for GUTSY.

Normal installation after checking that the compiler used for the kernel build is the present one (gcc version 4.1.3) logging out and 'Console loggin' to exit x-server
```
sudo apt-get install build-essential
wget http://download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86/1.0-7184/NVIDIA-Linux-x86-1.0-7184-pkg1.run
sudo sh NVIDIA-Linux-x86-1.0-7184-pkg1.run
```
leads to the known
```
ERROR: Unable to build the NVIDIA kernel module.
```
while config.h was not found since config.h was changed to /usr/src/linux-headers-2.6.22-14-generic/include/linux/autoconf.h.
See [http://www.linuxquestions.org/questions/linux-kernel-70/removal-of-includelinuxconfig.h-file-in-2.6.19-kernel-506363/]("http://www.linuxquestions.org/questions/linux-kernel-70/removal-of-includelinuxconfig.h-file-in-2.6.19-kernel-506363/").

The manual extraction and execution
```
sh NVIDIA-Linux-x86-1.0-7184-pkg1.run -x
cd NVIDIA-Linux-x86-1.0-7184-pkg1
sudo ./NVIDIA-Linux-x86-1.0-7184-pkg1
```
with the aim to change linux/config.h to linux/autoconf.h in ./NVIDIA-Linux-x86-1.0-7184-pkg1/usr/src/nv/nv-linux.h
leads to
```
ERROR: Unable to determine the NVIDIA kernel module filename.
```
even if no changes where made to the nv-linux.h or the extraction directory.

Even after
```
sudo apt-get install linux-kernel-devel
sudo updatedb
locate module.c
locate modules.c
```
gives no real mach to add the backward compatibility method mentioned in the link above (But I don't really understand what they say).

So the driver (which is and will stay forever the newest GLX supporting driver for nVidia Riva TNT2 M64) seams not installable anymore.

I would really appreciate for a solution without building a new kernel or the clear answer that this is not possible.

Thanks much

P.S.: Thanks to the ubuntu distributors!!! Great work!
